claims.text: Leveraging neural network training dynamics to generate calibrated uncertainty-aware pseudo-labels improves multi-view uncertainty estimation and robustness in medical image classification.
claims.best_result: Pseudo-D achieves best study-level AURC of 0.885 and MAE of 0.180

claims.text: We propose the Dynamic Contrastive Correction Network (DCCN) to correct uncertain regions in pseudo-labels for semi-supervised medical image segmentation, rather than discarding them.
A weakly supervised framework leveraging SAM's pseudo-label generation with uncertainty-aware cross-teaching can achieve accurate nodule segmentation using only aspect ratio annotations.
datasets: Thyroid Ultrasound Dataset (422 left + 422 right images)
Breast Ultrasound Dataset (604 left + 151 right images)
claims.best_result: Ours achieved DSC of 73.5±22.2 and HD95 of 32.8±71.5 on Thyroid Ultrasound, and DSC of 75.5±19.8 and HD95 of 52.3±36.5 on Breast Ultrasound

claims.text: A consistency regularization-based semi-supervised blood cell detection method, CRS-YOLOv5-ALT, is proposed to address the need for large amounts of labeled data.
Self-supervised pre-training combined with semi-supervised learning can effectively address label scarcity in medical imaging, enabling robust 3D object detection with limited annotations.
datasets: RSNA 2023 dataset (3,147 patients, 3,206 CT series for classification)
206 labeled CT volumes with 3D bounding box annotations for 3D injury detection (144 train, 30 val, 32 test)
2,000 unlabeled CT volumes for semi-supervised learning
claims.best_result: Semi-supervised learning achieved 56.57% mAP@0.50 (114% improvement) for 3D injury detection

claims.text: The proposed Mutual Evidential Deep Learning (MEDL) framework achieves state-of-the-art performance by generating reliable pseudo-labels through complementary evidence from dual architectures and uncertainty-guided learning.
claims.best_result: Ours: ACDC RV DSC 89.01%, JAC 80.80%, ASD 7.28mm, 95HD 1.70mm; LV DSC 93.34%, JAC 87.81%, ASD 4.25mm, 95HD 1.23mm; Avg DSC 90.40%, JAC 82.94%, ASD 3.38mm, 95HD 0.83mm

claims.text: SemiSeg-CAW is a semi-supervised segmentation framework that leverages class-level information and an adaptive multi-loss function to reduce dependency on extensive pixel-level annotations for ultrasound image segmentation.

datasets: ACDC (200 cardiac MRI images from 100 patients)
claims.best_result: SemSim achieved 0.896 DSC and 2.3mm 95HD on ACDC with 10% labeled data

claims.text: ARCO, a variance-reduced semi-supervised contrastive learning framework, improves robustness and label efficiency in medical image segmentation.

datasets: Automated Cardiac Diagnosis Challenge (ACDC) dataset (200 annotated short-axis cardiac MR-cine images from 100 patients)
claims.best_result: EVIL achieves DSC of 89.43% with 30% labeled data

claims.text: How to develop annotation-efficient deep learning to achieve accurate PHE segmentation is an open problem.
The Adaptive Blended Consistency Loss (ABCL) effectively counteracts data skew in imbalanced medical image classification by adaptively blending target class distributions based on class frequencies.
claims.best_result: UDA-WeightedCE-ABCL achieved UAR of 0.68, G-mean of 0.66, and average AUC of 0.96 on HAM10000 dataset

claims.text: A two-stage framework using feature representation learning with stage-adaptive contrastive methods and uncertainty-aware pseudo labeling achieves superior segmentation performance by creating compact and well-separated feature spaces.
datasets: Colon tumor dataset (5 labeled, 95 unlabeled scans)
claims.best_result: Ours achieves 79.81% Dice on Pancreas dataset (12 labeled, 48 unlabeled), surpassing state-of-the-art by 0.54%

claims.text: A lightweight architecture (MAKNet) trained with semi-supervised pseudo-labels achieves superior performance with 60-70% fewer parameters compared to popular architectures.
claims.best_result: 0.9403 AUC on hand-labeled test set for LesaNet (TCIA) on DeepLesion dataset

claims.text: The paper introduces a novel semi-supervised Strong-Teacher Consistency Network to address few-shot multi-class cardiac MRI image segmentation by leveraging abundant unlabelled data.

The Color-Structure Dual-Student (CSDS) framework achieves state-of-the-art performance in semi-supervised histopathology segmentation by learning disentangled representations of stain appearance and tissue structure.
claims.best_result: CSDS achieves Dice of 85.06 ± 0.57 and Jaccard of 74.87 ± 0.82 on GlaS with 10% labeled data

claims.text: This article proposes a physics-informed, uncertainty-aware dynamic balancing learning framework for unsupervised universal domain generalization (UUDG) in bearing fault diagnosis.

A weakly supervised segmentation framework using only four extreme points as annotation can achieve performance comparable to or surpassing fully supervised methods while significantly reducing annotation cost.
datasets: UNS (Ultrasound Nerve Segmentation) dataset with 2,323 image-mask pairs
claims.best_result: Our method achieved 67.70% IoU / 76.89% Dice on BUSI and 67.39% IoU / 79.12% Dice on UNS, surpassing fully supervised performance on BUSI

claims.text: The shared anatomy prior can be explicitly realized by incorporating an auxiliary unsupervised gross anatomy reconstruction task into a teacher-student semi-supervised segmentation framework.
The paper presents a novel semi-supervised medical segmentation framework called DCCLNet, which is based on deep consistent co-learning.
Self-training with data augmentation is an effective strategy for leveraging unlabeled data in semi-supervised medical image segmentation.
We propose a text-supervised, zero-shot approach for medical image segmentation that eliminates the need for human-annotated labels.
DRIVE introduces a dual-model framework with dual-robustness through information variability and entropic consistency to enhance generalization and robustness in SFUDA.
The paper proposes UT-MT, a semi-supervised model that addresses the scarcity of labeled data in 3D medical image segmentation.
Semi-supervised learning offers a more efficient alternative to supervised learning for cancer diagnostics by leveraging abundant unlabeled data, thus reducing the need for expensive manual labeling.
The objective of our study is to analyze how machine learning tools can be adapted for the automatic interpretation of abdominal ultrasound images.

Anomaly detection methods effectively address medical imaging tasks like classification and segmentation by leveraging semi-supervised and unsupervised learning, reducing dependence on costly labeled data.
datasets: CT scans of skeleton or spines

claims.text: An uncertainty-guided mutual consistency learning framework effectively exploits unlabeled data by integrating intra-task and cross-task consistency learning.
claims.best_result: UG-MCL achieved 90.16% Dice on LA dataset with 16 labeled scans and 64 unlabeled scans

claims.text: This paper proposes a semi-supervised learning method to address the problem of insufficient labeled data for medical image classification.
The paper proposes UADC, a method to improve semi-supervised segmentation by maintaining model diversity to prevent premature convergence.
We propose a novel two-stage framework for metric depth and pose estimation in colonoscopy that addresses scale ambiguity and domain gaps.
Infairness framework enables reliable fairness auditing with limited labeled data by combining small labeled datasets with large unlabeled datasets through semi-supervised inference.
claims.best_result: Infairness achieves lowest bias and variance for ∆TPR estimation on MIMIC-III

claims.text: Deep dictionary learning (DDL) is an effective image recognition method when working with few images available.
In this paper, to continually learn new categories using previous knowledge, we introduce class-incremental semantic segmentation of 3D point cloud.
Timestamp supervision (single timestamp per phase) with uncertainty-aware temporal diffusion achieves competitive performance to full supervision while reducing annotation cost by 74%.

Complementing SSL with an uncertainty-aware objective that quantifies both aleatoric and epistemic uncertainty improves performance by aligning uncertainty distributions between labeled and unlabeled domains.
We propose a semi-supervised FSS strategy that leverages additional prototypes from unlabeled images with uncertainty guided pseudo label refinement.
datasets: PASCAL-5i (20 classes, 4 folds, 5 classes per fold)
COCO-20i (80 classes, 4 folds, 20 classes per fold)
claims.best_result: Ours + Iq + R achieves 56.27% mean-IoU (1-shot) and 64.73% (5-shot) on PASCAL-5i with M=12 unlabeled images

claims.text: The Uncertainty-aware Distillation with Class-Equilibrium (UaD-CE) framework effectively resolves Semi-FSCIL challenges by combining class-balanced self-training and uncertainty-aware distillation.
claims.best_result: UaD-CE achieves 54.50% on CIFAR100, 50.52% on miniImageNet, and 60.72% on CUB200 in the final session

claims.text: This paper presents UPE, an uncertainty-aware and parameter-efficient self-training method to improve the performance of large pre-trained language models in low-resource scenarios.
Self-supervised and semi-supervised learning are effective approaches for multi-sequence medical data analysis.

This paper proposes an Active Learning method to improve cross-domain semantic segmentation by selecting informative pixels for annotation, thereby mitigating the issue of noisy pseudo-labels and reducing the performance gap with fully-supervised models.
The paper proposes a conformal credal self-supervised learning method that addresses the lack of validity in pseudo-labels by using sets of probability distributions instead of single ones.
Aggregated Mutual Learning between CNN and Transformer models can effectively improve performance in semi-supervised medical image analysis.
Integrating natural language descriptions with a Mixture-of-Experts architecture significantly improves the performance of semi-supervised medical image segmentation.
This research introduces a method for semi-supervised medical image segmentation by transferring semantic knowledge.
This paper proposes PTCL, a pseudo-label temporal curriculum learning method to enable dynamic node classification in scenarios with limited and delayed labels.
This paper introduces a novel pseudo-labeling method that is both uncertainty-aware and adaptive to improve referring video object segmentation.
A novel semi-supervised adversarial self-integration network is proposed to overcome the limitations of traditional U-Net architectures.
This paper presents a novel Complex-to-Simple (CTS) framework to transfer models.

The paper proposes the SS-Net to address challenges in semi-supervised medical image segmentation by applying smoothness constraints and encouraging class-level separation.

This paper proposes a Momentum Contrastive Voxel-wise Representation Distillation method for effective semi-supervised learning.
We propose HiProto, a method that uses hierarchical prototype learning to achieve interpretable object detection, especially under low-quality imaging conditions.
This paper proposes a new method for few-shot open-set object detection that uses evidential uncertainty from a Dirichlet distribution to improve performance in data-scarce scenarios.
Decoupled Semantic Prototypes enable learning from diverse annotation types, making segmentation more affordable for expert-driven domains like medical imaging.
The paper claims that combining synthetic data with self-supervision can effectively address the domain shift problem in cross-domain crowd counting.
MedGS is a novel method that uses Gaussian Splatting to effectively represent and visualize multi-modal 3D medical images.
This paper presents a method for uncertainty-aware adaptation in self-supervised 3D human pose estimation.
The paper argues that the standard weak-to-strong augmentation strategy is ineffective for source-free domain adaptive object detection.
